_comment: Base model compatibility matrix for TheOrc fine-tuning.

models.reason: Observed planning failures directly. Fine-tuning impact is highest. Already in ModelProfiles.
models._unsloth_note: Unsloth has a native Gemma4 fast kernel. Use FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained() with model_name='google/gemma-4-12b-it'.

vram._note: LoRA in bf16 requires the full bf16 base (~24GB unquantized). Unsloth's optimizations bring this to ~18GB practical.

qat_variant._note: Google's official QAT Q4_0 GGUF — installed 2026-06-09. theorc-boss:gemma4 now uses this as base. Pull command: ollama pull hf.co/google/gemma-4-12B-it-qat-q4_0-gguf:Q4_0

models._gain_note: Moves from BossScore 2 (raw) to estimated 7-8 after 300-example LoRA. The planning collapse is a training distribution gap — fine-tuning directly addresses it.
known_issues: thinking_mode_consumes_tokens: Fixed via think=false in Modelfile (Ollama 0.30+)
token_leakage_via_openai_compat: Fixed via StripGemma4Artifacts() in SwarmSession
default_temperature_too_high: Fixed via temperature=0.2 in Modelfile

models.reason: QAT base preserves quality at 4-bit better than standard quantization. Prefer once Ollama tag exists.

vram._note: Slightly lower QLoRA requirement because the QAT quantization is more VRAM-efficient.

models.upgrade_path: When available: pull via 'ollama pull gemma4:12b-it-qat', then update theorc-boss-gemma4.Modelfile FROM line and rebuild via 'ollama create theorc-boss:gemma4 -f theorc-boss-gemma4.Modelfile'

models.reason: Already performs well as boss (BossScore 6). Fine-tuning offers marginal gains. Worth doing after gemma4 to generalize the fine-tune approach.
models._gain_note: Already works. Fine-tuning would push BossScore from 6 to 8-9 and reduce planning variance on complex goals.

models.reason: Highest base capability. Fine-tuning cost/benefit is poor — already scores well.
models._note: Requires A6000/A100 for QLoRA. Out of scope for consumer hardware fine-tuning.

models.reason: 18 GB inference model. Fine-tuning requires 40+ GB VRAM. Not viable on consumer hardware.

unsloth.reason: Native Gemma4 fast kernel, 2x faster than PEFT, 40% less VRAM, direct GGUF export
unsloth.install: pip install unsloth[cu124] -- for CUDA 12.4; see unsloth.ai/docs for version matrix
unsloth._note: Unsloth changed their package name in 2025. Current install is 'pip install unsloth' not 'pip install unsloth[colab]'.

peft_trl.reason: No Gemma4-specific optimizations. Higher VRAM. Use as fallback only.
peft_trl.install: pip install peft trl bitsandbytes transformers

gguf_export_path.unsloth: model.save_pretrained_merged('merged/', tokenizer, save_method='merged_4bit') then model.save_pretrained_gguf('gguf_output/', tokenizer, quantization_method='q4_k_m')
gguf_export_path._note: Unsloth's save_pretrained_gguf merges the LoRA adapter and quantizes in one step. Output is a single .gguf file ready for Ollama.
